Meet our founder

A Message From Our Founder

Esther Oluwafeyisayo Olafisoye

Founder & Executive Director
Hadassah Initiative for Empowerment Foundation

Esther Oluwafeyisayo Olafisoye is the visionary founder of Hadassah Initiative for Empowerment Foundation, driven by an unwavering passion for humanitarian service, women empowerment, and youth development. Her leadership has transformed the foundation into a trusted platform for community transformation — built on compassion, conviction, and a deep commitment to creating positive social impact.

“

Every life touched, every child empowered, and every community transformed brings us closer to a better world.

Read Her Full Story

Years of Service
Who we are

A Foundation Built on Years of Trust

Hadassah Foundation began as a small neighborhood initiative and has grown into a trusted humanitarian organization, recorded — program by program, year by year — in the communities we serve. Every entry in our story is a family helped, a child educated, a skill learned.

  • Faith-rooted, community-led approach to development
  • Transparent allocation of every donation received
  • Programs designed with and for the people we serve
Our Full Story
Our Work
What we do

Six Programs, One Mission

Every program is a different door into the same purpose: helping people build a better life, on their own terms.

Youth Empowerment

Mentorship, leadership training, and life-skills coaching that help young people discover and pursue their potential.

Learn more

Skills Acquisition

Vocational training in tailoring, ICT, and trades that build real, income-generating capability.

Learn more

Community Outreach

Direct, on-the-ground support for underserved neighborhoods through visits, drives, and partnerships.

Learn more

Education Support

Scholarships, school supplies, and tutoring that keep children in classrooms and on track.

Learn more

Humanitarian Assistance

Emergency relief — food, shelter, and essentials — for families facing crisis or displacement.

Learn more

Support for Vulnerable Individuals

Targeted care for the elderly, persons with disabilities, and individuals in crisis.

Learn more
A record of impact

14+ Years of Transforming Lives

From a single outreach program in 2012 to a network of programs reaching 25+ communities today, every year has added a new entry to our record of service — verified by the families and communities we've walked alongside.

Stories of change

Real Impact, Real People

Empowering Women Through Skills Training

Over 150 women successfully completed vocational training programs, enabling them to start small businesses and improve their household income.

Supporting Children's Education

The foundation provided educational materials, scholarships, and mentorship support to vulnerable children, helping them remain in school.

Community Outreach and Healthcare

Medical outreach programs provided free health screenings, health education, and essential support services to underserved communities.

See Our Full Impact
